item.title: Iran launches fresh strikes across Gulf
item.description: Iran launched strikes across the Gulf on Sunday after vowing to avenge slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, defying a threat from president Donald Trump to strike with unprecedented force....

item.title: Passengers in pain as HSIA cancels 40 more Gulf flights
item.description: Hundreds of Gulf Arab states-bound passengers got stranded and immensely suffered at the Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka on Sunday as 40 more flights were cancelled to and from the airport on the day....

item.title: Lalon festival begins in Kushtia today
item.description: The annual Lalon festival at Chheuriya Akhrabari in Kumarakhali upazila of Kushtia, traditionally held over three days on the occasion of Dol Purnima, will be observed for two days this year due to Ramadan....

item.title: 2,000 fake certificates identified among teachers
item.description: Around 2,000 fake certificates belonging to teachers working at educational institutions have been identified, according to the Directorate of Inspection and Audit that detected the counterfeit documents in recent years....

item.title: Security of foreign missions in Dhaka enhanced
item.description: Bangladesh government on Sunday enhanced security measures for diplomatic missions of foreign countries in Dhaka amid escalating tensions in the Gulf Arab states following attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran and retaliatory strikes on US bases......

item.title: Pentagon says three US service members killed in Iran operation
item.description: The US military on Sunday said three service members have been killed and five seriously wounded in the war against Iran – the first casualties announced on the US side....

item.title: More flights cancelled as Iran conflict shuts ME hubs
item.description: The biggest disruption to global air transport since the Covid pandemic continued Sunday, with thousands of flights affected and busy Middle Eastern hubs including Dubai and Doha shuttered as Iran lashed out after US-Israeli strikes....

item.title: Will upper house deliver?
item.description: THE long-debated proposal for a bicameral parliament has moved from abstract discourse to constitutional reality. The signing of the July charter, the promulgation of an ordinance establishing provisions for an upper chamber and a national referendum that included a question on its creation have collectively transformed the institutional landscape. What was once......

item.title: Militancy, sovereignty at South Asia’s fault line
item.description: THE latest escalation between Pakistan and Afghanistan marks not merely another episode of cross-border violence, but the visible collapse of a fragile equilibrium that had held, uneasily, since the Taliban’s return to power in Kabul. What unfolded over the past week — airstrikes, retaliatory assaults and declarations invoking expanded military operations......

item.title: Smarter drug policy beyond crackdown
item.description: BANGLADESH is once again in the midst of a sweeping anti-drug campaign. Law enforcement agencies, including the Bangladesh Police and the Rapid Action Battalion, are conducting operations across cities and districts, arresting suspected dealers and users in what officials describe as a ‘zero tolerance’ drive. The stated goal is clear: protect communities from the......

item.title: The grim choice made
item.description: THE American and Israeli bombs and missiles that fell on Iran on March 1 did not merely target nuclear facilities. The marked the point of no return for a region already perched on the edge of the abyss. In a March 2025 analysis, I raised the question of whether Washington would ultimately choose military action or accept a nuclear-capable Iran. That question has now......

item.title: Time for govt to move fast to curb violence, extortion
item.description: VIOLENCE has continued to make the headlines despite the government’s commitment to prioritising law and order. The firing at the house of an industrialist in Chattogram shows how heavily extortion weighs on the business community. CCTV footage shows four masked men firing dozens of shots from a canal bank behind the house early February 28. The house was......

item.title: US must not violate other states’ sovereignty
item.description: THE US-Israel attacks on Iran — which began on February 28 and have left more than 130 dead, including Iran’s supreme leader and some members of the family, and more than 200 wounded in about 24 hours — have laid waste to the rule-based international order. The order, a post-World War II framework of laws, was designed to manage state relations and prevent......

item.title: Russian missile attacks on Ukraine hit 3-year high in Feb: AFP
item.description: Russia fired more missiles at Ukraine in February than in any other month since at least the beginning of 2023 in overnight strikes, targeting Ukrainian energy infrastructure in particular, an AFP analysis showed Sunday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Two workers die, falling from building
item.description: Two construction workers were killed after they had fallen from a building under construction at Anwara upazila of Chattogram on Sunday....

item.title: Pak, Afghan forces battle along border
item.description: Afghan and Pakistani troops battled along their border, Afghan residents and officials said on Sunday, with the fighting coming alongside multiple strikes including the former US air base at Bagram....

item.title: ACC to sue Sheikh Selim for Tk 13 crore illegal wealth
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission on Sunday approved the filing of a case against Sheikh Fazlul Karim Selim, former minister and presidium member of Bangladesh Awami League which is currently banned from holding activities, on charges of amassing illegal wealth....

item.title: Youth killed by roommate after altercation: police
item.description: An altercation over a loud phone conversation allegedly led to the murder of a youth, whose body parts were recovered from at least four locations inDhaka, by his roommate, the police said on Sunday....Daily news updates

item.title: One injured in Cox’s Bazar filling station fire dies
item.description: A 28-year-old man who sustained burn injuries in a fire following an explosion at an LPG filling station in the Kolatoli area of Cox’s Bazar town died on Sunday....

item.title: Scholars’ School and College students shine in junior scholarship examination
item.description: Students of Scholars’ School & College have achieved an exceptional success in the Junior Scholarship Examination for Class 8, securing an impressive 42 out of 104 scholarships in the Dhanmondi police station region, says a press release....

item.title: Teen domestic worker scalded in Barishal
item.description: A teenage domestic worker was allegedly scalded with hot water at Mehendiganj in Barishal, prompting police to arrest the accused woman and send her to jail....

item.title: Initiative taken to introduce Health Card for journalists
item.description: Managing director of the Bangladesh Journalists Welfare Trust Muhammad Abdullah on Sunday said that an initiative was taken to introduce a ‘Health Card’ for journalists to ensure structured and sustainable medical support....Bangladesh politics insights

item.title: Ferry services resume on Paturia-Daulatdia route after 3.5 hours
item.description: Ferry services on the Paturia-Daulatdia route resumed after remaining suspended for three and a half hours due to dense fog....

item.title: Day labourer family shocked as fire kills 21 goats, two cows
item.description: A day labourer’s family has been left devastated after fire gutted their home and killed 21 goats and two cows at Babuganj in Barishal early Sunday....

item.title: Activists demand revival of Barohi, Nabaganga rivers
item.description: Environmental activists and researchers on Sunday demanded immediate measures to revive Barohi and Nabaganga, two nearly dead rivers in Rajshahi city by stopping encroachment and pollution and by dredging their beds....

item.title: Flag Hoisting Day today
item.description: Today is Flag Hoisting Day, the day when Bangladesh’s national flag was hoisted for the first time on the campus of the University of Dhaka in 1971....

item.title: Total lunar eclipse tomorrow
item.description: A total eclipse of the moon will occur on Tuesday, according to a press release of the Inter-Services Public Relations directorate, issued Sunday afternoon....

item.title: Some Iranians celebrate after reports of Khamenei death
item.description: Iranians took to the streets cheering with joy and playing celebratory music after reports of Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s death, according to video footage verified by AFP....
